meta.homeTitle: Alpzeit · Apartments on the Arlberg in Schnann
meta.homeDescription: Three apartments on the quiet side of the Arlberg. Ten minutes from the Galzigbahn, an hour from Innsbruck airport. Book direct.

about.label: ● About us
about.body: Three apartments on the quiet side of the Arlberg. Alpzeit is a small family house in Schnann, ten minutes from the Galzigbahn but a world away from the crowds. We rent it the way we'd want to stay ourselves — unhurried, understated, and always with the mountains in view.

items.label: To the Galzigbahn
items.description: Just ten minutes from the Arlberg's main cable car — close enough for first tracks, far enough for real quiet in the evening.

items.label: From Innsbruck airport
items.description: A single drive through the Inn valley. No transfers, no detours.

items.label: Days of snow per year
items.description: One of the most reliable snow regions in the Alps.

location.heading: Schnann. Easy to reach. Hard to leave.
location.sub: A single-street village in the Stanzertal. Five minutes by bus to St. Anton. One hour by car from Innsbruck airport.

stats.value: Walk to the village bus stop (line 4242, every 30 min).
Bus ride to the St. Anton Galzigbahn gondola.
By car or train from Innsbruck airport (INN).

arlberg.heading: The Arlberg, from the doorstep.
arlberg.sub: 305 km of pistes. 88 lifts. Ski-in on the bus; ski back to the apartment door by 16:30.

items.q: How far is Alpzeit from the ski lifts?
items.a: The Galzigbahn — the main cable car into the St. Anton ski area — is a ten-minute drive from Schnann. During the ski season, a free ski bus stops in the village several times a day.

items.q: Is direct booking better than booking through an agency?
items.a: Yes. When you book directly you pay no agency commission, which means a lower rate for the same apartment. You also deal with us directly for any questions or requests.
The nightly rate includes final cleaning, bed linen and towels, and Austrian tourist tax. There are no hidden fees.

items.q: Can we arrive in summer, not just winter?
items.a: Absolutely. Schnann and the Stanzertal valley are beautiful year-round. Summer brings hiking, mountain biking, and the Verwallsee lake. Several guests return specifically for the quieter summer season.
During peak ski season (Christmas–New Year and February school holidays) the minimum stay is seven nights. All other periods are flexible — from three nights.

items.q: Is there parking at the property?
items.a: Yes, each apartment has a dedicated parking space. In winter the parking area is regularly cleared of snow.
Cancellations up to 30 days before arrival are fully refunded. Between 30 and 14 days, 50% is refunded. Inside 14 days the full amount is charged. We recommend travel insurance for peace of mind.
Well-behaved dogs are welcome in the Lodge and the Hearth with prior notice. A small cleaning supplement applies. Please mention this when you enquire.

footer.bestRate: The best rate is always here.
footer.bookDirectSub: Book directly with us — no agency, no commission, no markup.

newsletter.heading: Seasonal updates and quiet weeks
newsletter.thanks: Thank you — we'll be in touch.

booking.emailConfirm: We'll email confirmation within the hour.

lodge: Spread across two floors, The Lodge is the largest of the three apartments. The kitchen opens onto a long wooden dining table that comfortably seats twelve, with two separate sitting rooms for quiet evenings and louder ones.
Five bedrooms share three bathrooms. A wood-burning stove in the main living room, large south-facing windows onto the mountains, and a private entrance from the side of the house.
[Placeholder — to be filled in by the owner.]
hearth: The Hearth is a two-floor apartment of wood and stone. An open kitchen with a central island, three bedrooms, and a south-facing balcony that gets the afternoon sun.
Designed for a family or two couples. The living room has a wood-burning stove and direct access to the terrace, with views across the valley.
[Placeholder — to be filled in by the owner.]
eyrie: The Eyrie sits at the top of the building — a top-floor studio with a skylight over the bed. In clear weather, the Arlberg peaks line up through it.
A quiet space for one or two people. Small but thoughtfully arranged, with a fully equipped kitchenette, a fold-out desk, and more light than the floor plan suggests.
[Placeholder — to be filled in by the owner.]

apartmentDescriptions.lodge: The largest of the three. Generous kitchen, two sitting rooms, a long wooden table that seats all twelve.
apartmentDescriptions.hearth: Two floors of wood and stone. Open kitchen, three bedrooms, the balcony faces south.
apartmentDescriptions.eyrie: A top-floor studio. Skylight over the bed; in clear weather, the Arlberg peaks line up through it.
